model_output: Perfect! I found exactly 5 confirmed entries. Now let me sort them alphabetically and compute the SHA-1 hash:

args.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import hashlib

# Extracted values from confirmed entries (regional verification panel)
data = {
    'Vyport': '92.4 dB',
    'Saltmoor': '88.0 dB',
    'Wenmoor': '104.7 dB',
    'Carrick': '76.3 dB',
    'Halewick': '111.2 dB'
}

# Sort alphabetically by entity name
sorted_pairs = sorted(data.items())

# Format as entity=value joined by |
joined = '|'.join(f"{entity}={value}" for entity, value in sorted_pairs)

print("Sorted pairs:", sorted_pairs)
print("Joined string:", joined)

# Compute SHA-1 hash (lowercase hex, UTF-8 encoding, no trailing newline)
hash_result = hashlib.sha1(joined.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest()
print("SHA-1 hash:", hash_result)
EOF
args.description: Compute SHA-1 hash of sorted entity-value pairs

tool_returns.detail:

Joined string: Carrick=76.3 dB|Halewick=111.2 dB|Saltmoor=88.0 dB|Vyport=92.4 dB|Wenmoor=104.7 dB
